review_text: What is there to be said? It's a classic, one of the best movies ever made, and its a downright crime that the tomatometer and popcornmeter are below 100%.
My favorite Christmas movie. Such an interesting concept to consider in our own lives.

A perennial classic and for a reason. While initially a box office flop due to its high costs and release date it has been vindicated by television and home video. "It's a Wonderful Life" tells the story of a man named George Bailey who after some hardships wish he was never born. Now an angel has been sent to Earth to show him just how different everything would be without his presence. The quintessential film about an existential crisis. What makes this movie a good one is it's cast including James Stewart and Donna Reed whose on-screen chemistry steals the entire movie. It's an emotional holiday film, and it will tug on your heartstrings. "Attaboy, Clarence!"

Always my favorite movie to watch on Christmas Eve with the family. If you still plan to watch it even after Christmas, make sure you watch the version that says "Black and White" in the title; this one is the unedited version that keeps all the original parts. All other versions on Prime edit it in some way that takes away a lot of the experience. Do yourself a favor and watch it in its full glory. If we ever get a decent edit, I'll be the first to let you know.
I'll never thing about killing myself ever again

The fact that my family says this movie is boring, is almost as sad as the middle of this movie. Truly a cinematic masterpiece, and one of the greatest films of all time. Holds up now, and will until the end of time.

Anyway, it's a good movie. I like it.

Saccharine at times, deeply profound the next. The ending is predictable, but we love it nonetheless. The greatest Christmas movie of the 20th century. Always worth a watch with family.

Well written, well acted, smart script, bittersweet, and joyous ending. This movie has held up for 80 years. Among many Christmas classic movies, this, for me, has endured as the finest of the lot, and frankly one of the best movies of all time. It traces the tribulations of life we all go through, and provides a valuable reminder that, in the end, we rely on God for everything. By serving God through service unto others, we find happiness and a wonderful life. And those who, like Potter, only serve themselves by taking from others and avoiding opportunities to be generous, serve themselves a steaming plate of misery and bitterness.

A truly morale tale that's as relevant now as it was when it was made all those decades ago. A timeless, hugely enjoyable family classic that covers benevolence, capitolustic malevolence, friendship, family values, an enduring love story a man's dispair, redemption, the true meaning of life, community spirit, with a sprinkling of Christmas magic, ending in a few tears of joy.

Quite possibly the most pure & uplifting film I’ve ever seen with an overwhelming perspective on life. I’m a sucker for a decade spanning look at a character & the screenplay scratches all my itches. It’s cartoonish & theatrical at times but that drives home the joyous charm of it all. The concept is simple but packs an insane punch by showing the unfathomable impact living a selfless life can have on others. The message of treating those around you with kindness & compassion is universal & the sense of community fills my spirit with something indescribable.
One of the holiday traditions with my family
Holiday staple or not, It’s a Wonderful Life still hits. It’s not just cozy it’s a masterclass in showing how one life, lived with heart, can change everything.
Favorite classic Christmas movie.

A holiday classic that is really a gripping drama about one's man's life. It's a Wonderful Life (1946) is director Frank Capra's masterpiece of humanistic drama. He revels into the simple pleasures of family, kindness, giving, and decency. It's a Wonderful Life carries a timeless message of appreciating what you have in life. Capra's direction is marvelous. James Stewart is the protagonist George Bailey, whom we follow closely from adolescence to adulthood. Stewart is fascinating as the relatable everyman. You like him and want him to succeed because of his decency, but empathize with his pain as it all comes crumbling down around him. Donna Reed is similarly magnificent. She is as lovely and charming as any young leading actress, but so sincere in her love for George. You hope for her happiness and Reed demonstrates a playful, kind, loyal, and thoughtful wife as any man could hope to marry one day. Reed captures the magic of real love on film in It's a Wonderful Life.
Best movie ever made.
great feel good movie that teaches you what is important in life
